name: CLI entry fast paths and respawn shell
summary: Two-stage launcher that validates Node/build output, answers root help/version cheaply, normalizes startup argv/env, and respawns only when Node flags or CA-cert settings must change.

name: Lazy Commander graph and plugin CLI injection
summary: Command graph that registers only the needed core/sub-CLI surfaces and injects plugin-owned command groups from validated config when required by the invocation.

name: Config IO pipeline with runtime snapshots
summary: JSON5 config loader/writer with include expansion, env substitution, validation, snapshot hashing, runtime cache refresh, and write-back preservation for env references.

name: Session keying and lock-safe session store runtime
summary: Session identity, filesystem path resolution, transcript persistence, and lock-safe store mutation for per-agent session state under the OpenClaw state directory.

name: SecretRef contract and bounded resolver
summary: Reusable env/file/exec secret reference grammar plus resolver with provider defaults, concurrency limits, filesystem safety guards, and typed resolution errors.

name: Lazy library/runtime facade
summary: Dual-use package surface that keeps CLI entry lean while exposing import-time library APIs through lazy runtime module loading and a shared runtime output contract.

name: Gateway bootstrap and runtime assembly
summary: Long-lived control-plane bootstrap that prepares auth/secrets/config, selects startup plugins, resolves bind and UI policy, creates HTTP and WebSocket runtime state, and wires discovery, sidecars, cron, and maintenance.

name: Gateway request-dispatch envelope
summary: Method dispatch shell that authorizes gateway RPC calls, enforces write budgets, wraps handler execution in gateway request scope, and exposes the canonical method and event inventory.

name: Gateway chat/session fanout runtime
summary: Live chat/session event plane with active-run tracking, subscriber registries, tool-event recipients, and WS attachment for transcript, agent, and node-adjacent events.

name: Gateway plugin and channel bridge
summary: Gateway-side bridge that loads startup plugins, exposes internal gateway dispatch to plugin subagents, and manages channel accounts with restart policy and optional plugin runtime helpers.

name: Gateway node registry and subscription plane
summary: Connected-node registry with invoke request tracking plus a small dual-index subscription manager for sending session-scoped or global events only to relevant nodes.

name: Gateway control UI HTTP surface
summary: Safe static serving layer for the browser control UI with bootstrap JSON, avatar indirection, CSP headers, base-path handling, and SPA fallback.

name: Session-key routing bridge
summary: Helper pack for normalizing agent IDs and translating between request keys, store keys, peer keys, main keys, and thread-specific session variants across gateway, channels, and sessions.

name: Gateway tools catalog handler
summary: Operator-facing RPC that merges core tool sections and plugin-provided tools into a grouped catalog scoped to a selected agent workspace.

name: Gateway skills control-plane handlers
summary: RPC handlers for workspace skill status, search, detail, install, and config-backed update flows across local and ClawHub skill sources.

name: ACP session manager and runtime options
summary: ACP control-plane manager with runtime caching, actor-queued turn execution, runtime option normalization, and session observability over ACP-backed sessions.

name: Generic plugin entry DSL
summary: Small author-facing DSL that normalizes plugin declarations, defers config-schema evaluation, and keeps registration logic outside core bootstrap code.

name: Single-provider plugin entry DSL
summary: Provider-specific authoring helper that adds env-var aggregation, auth method scaffolding, onboarding wizard metadata, and default catalog generation.

name: Bundled channel entry loader
summary: Boundary-safe loader for bundled channel plugins that resolves runtime/setup sidecars and can fall back from built output to source trees.

name: Channel setup-plugin shell
summary: Setup-only channel plugin composition that reuses one platform base builder, exports onboarding/status surfaces, and avoids starting the live monitor/runtime loop.

name: Channel plugin composition template
summary: Reference template for full channel plugin assembly that merges platform base, setup, routing, security, approvals, directory, and runtime surfaces into one createChatChannelPlugin declaration.

name: Channel monitor and provider loops
summary: Long-lived provider monitors that translate external SDK events into inbound messages, approvals, typing, and runtime status for channel-backed sessions.

name: Channel target normalization and outbound routing pack
summary: Per-platform helpers that normalize messaging targets and translate them into stable outbound session routes or delivery identities.

name: Channel thread-binding managers
summary: Durable conversation-binding managers that keep an external thread or room attached to one OpenClaw session with sweeper and lifecycle policy.

name: Channel doctor and security policy surfaces
summary: Operator-facing setup, doctor, security-audit, and runtime group-policy helpers that keep channel adapters diagnosable and safer to configure.

name: Channel registerFull sidecar hooks
summary: Extension entry sidecars that lazily bolt on HTTP routes, gateway methods, framework commands, or subagent hooks without enlarging the core channel plugin contract.

name: Plugin registration capture testkit
summary: Narrow test harness that builds a registration API and captures providers, tools, CLI hooks, and other declarations without starting the full host.

name: Plugin runtime facade and scoped registry bootstrap
summary: Injected runtime surface plus registry bootstrap helpers for plugin-owned code, including lazy host services and optional gateway-bound subagent access.

name: Plugin SDK package bridges
summary: Curated package-export layer that keeps stable public subpaths while canonical implementations live in the main source tree.

name: Memory host SDK package bridge
summary: Thin package wrapper that publishes memory-host engine subpaths while the real implementation stays under src/memory-host-sdk.

name: Embedded run coordinator and compaction shell
summary: Outer embedded-run loop that backfills session keys, resolves workspace/auth/model state, coordinates retries and live model switching, and runs compaction plus post-compaction side effects around individual attempts.

name: Embedded attempt prompt and tool loop
summary: Single-attempt execution kernel that assembles system prompts, session manager state, tool definitions, MCP and LSP runtimes, stream transports, sandbox context, and tool-result guards.

name: Tool inventory composition shell
summary: Host-side assembly layer that builds shipped tools, gates them by provider and sandbox state, and appends plugin-provided tools without hardwiring the final inventory into the runner core.

name: Subagent spawn, registry, and control runtime
summary: Lifecycle machinery for spawning subagents, persisting run metadata, recovering orphaned runs, and exposing list, kill, steer, and session-bound control flows.

name: Auth-profile store, ordering, and cooldown policy
summary: Credential-profile persistence plus eligibility ordering, external auth overlays, and cooldown or failure accounting so providers can rotate across multiple auth identities.

name: Model selection and managed catalog materialization
summary: Model-ref normalization, alias and allowlist building, per-agent or per-subagent default resolution, and managed `models.json` generation driven by config and runtime snapshots.

name: Model fallback executor
summary: Retry and failover shell that runs a caller-supplied operation against multiple model candidates while preserving summary errors and throttling recovery probes.

name: Agent command and auto-reply bridge
summary: Integration layer that exposes the embedded runtime safely to CLI, ingress, and auto-reply callers while handling trust defaults, session bookkeeping, delivery, and follow-up behavior.

name: Browser automation plugin
summary: Pluginized browser runtime with server context, routes and command surfaces rather than a one-off script bundle.

name: Hybrid memory index manager
summary: Memory subsystem combining filesystem/session sync, hybrid FTS-vector search, embedding provider abstraction and cache management.

name: Provider bundle entry aggregator
summary: Extension entry pattern that publishes several provider-owned capabilities such as models, speech, media understanding, search, or generation from one plugin boundary.

name: Provider auth and discovery overlays
summary: Plugin-owned policy layer for auth modes, discovery, stream wrappers, replay hooks, and forward-compat model resolution on top of the generic provider entry DSL.

name: Browser plugin registration shell
summary: Thin browser plugin seam that wires tool, CLI, gateway method, node-host commands, and a lazy background service into the host plugin API.

name: Browser gateway proxy and profile context
summary: Profile-scoped browser request layer that chooses local versus node-proxied execution, resolves browser profiles, and persists proxy files back into host-visible paths.

name: Memory runtime and tool shell
summary: Memory plugin layer that exports runtime lookup, memory_search and memory_get tools, prompt-section injection, and transcript flush planning without exposing the full engine directly.

name: Short-term memory promotion engine
summary: Heuristic subsystem that records recall hits, ranks short-term snippets by frequency and recency, and promotes candidates into durable memory artifacts.

name: Media-understanding registry and runner
summary: Attachment-oriented orchestration layer that builds provider registries, resolves scope and entry order, and runs provider or CLI backends per media capability.

name: Capability-generation runtime shared helpers
summary: Generic helper pack for capability runtimes that resolves provider-model candidates, normalizes size or aspect overrides, and throws consistent failover errors.

name: SSRF-aware media store
summary: Temporary media persistence layer for local or remote inputs with mime sniffing, redirect-safe download, TTL cleanup, and buffer-path resolution.

name: TTS lazy facade loader
summary: Manual facade that exposes speech runtime APIs through lazy loading of the activated bundled speech-core public surface.

name: Control UI gateway console
summary: Operator console for sessions, chat, config, skills, nodes and approvals over the OpenClaw gateway contracts.

name: Control UI bootstrap identity loader
summary: Base-path-aware bootstrap fetch that loads assistant identity and control-UI metadata before the browser socket handshake completes.

name: Control UI browser gateway client
summary: Browser WebSocket/RPC client with operator-scoped hello handshake, request tracking, sequence-gap handling, device identity signing, and one-shot device-token retry.

name: Control UI schema config-form stack
summary: Schema-driven config editor that normalizes gateway JSON schema into a renderable subset, supports section search and diffs, and preserves raw-mode fallback for unsupported paths.

name: Control UI chat slash-command and speech stack
summary: Browser chat surface that combines gateway-backed chat state, grouped transcript rendering, local slash-command execution, command completion metadata, and optional STT/TTS affordances.

name: Control UI operator panels for skills, nodes, approvals, sessions, and usage
summary: Collection of gateway-backed operator panels that wrap RPC into searchable skill management, node and exec-approval editing, session tables, and usage reporting.

name: TUI gateway chat client
summary: Gateway-backed terminal client that resolves auth from config and explicit flags, opens the protocol session, and exposes chat, session, agent, and model RPC helpers.

name: TUI session, command, and event shell
summary: Terminal-side shell that maps keyboard and slash-like commands into gateway operations, assembles streaming events into chat output, and keeps session or agent state synchronized.

name: Shared Swift gateway and chat kit
summary: Shared Swift package carrying protocol models, gateway helpers, command DTOs and chat UI reused by iOS and macOS clients.

name: Swift gateway protocol DTO pack
summary: Generated Swift contract layer for websocket frames, snapshots, sessions, config, tools, and node RPC payloads consumed by Apple clients.

name: Swift gateway channel, auth recovery, and TLS pinning
summary: Apple websocket runtime with signed device identity, typed connect errors, request tracking, keepalive, reconnect policy, and fingerprint-pinned TLS sessions.

name: Swift gateway node-session bridge
summary: Node-oriented wrapper over the shared gateway channel that handles invoke requests, snapshots, canvas capability URLs, and bridge event fan-out.

name: Swift chat transport and UI kit
summary: Shared Apple chat kit built around a small transport protocol, a reusable chat state machine, transcript decoding, and a SwiftUI chat surface.

name: Swift tool display registry
summary: Resource-backed helper that converts tool names and args into concise emoji, label, verb, and detail summaries for Apple UI surfaces.

name: Swift talk directive and prompt helper pack
summary: Small helper pack for voice-mode prompting that parses first-line JSON directives, selects normalized provider config, and builds spoken-mode prompt text.

name: Swift deep-link and setup-code parser
summary: Safe parser for `openclaw://agent` and `openclaw://gateway` routes plus base64url setup-code decoding with loopback-only plaintext rules.

name: Swabble wake-word gate
summary: Generic wake-word detector that scans timestamped speech segments, enforces a minimum post-trigger gap, and extracts the command text that follows the trigger.

name: macOS menu bar companion
summary: Desktop companion that supervises the local gateway, hosts panels and surfaces exec approvals/onboarding in a native menu bar app.

name: Desktop IPC request contract
summary: Typed macOS-local IPC contract for permissions, notifications, shell, canvas, node actions, and media capture used by desktop surfaces instead of ad hoc payloads.

name: macOS gateway endpoint and tunnel stack
summary: Desktop runtime that resolves local versus remote gateway endpoints, starts or reuses SSH control tunnels, and exposes one shared gateway websocket connection with retry helpers.

name: macOS launchd gateway supervisor
summary: Attach-or-start supervisor that detects an existing local gateway, inspects launchd state, and drives the OpenClaw CLI to install, restart, or disable the gateway service.

name: macOS gateway discovery with wide-area and Tailscale fallbacks
summary: Discovery model that merges Bonjour browsing with wide-area DNS-SD and Tailscale Serve probes into one deduped list of candidate gateways.

name: macOS exec-approval socket and policy bridge
summary: Native approval subsystem that receives exec requests from the gateway or a local socket, evaluates allowlist and security policy, and presents desktop prompts before host execution.

name: macOS web chat panel wrapper
summary: Desktop wrapper that adapts the shared SwiftUI chat kit to macOS windows and anchored panels through a gateway-backed transport.

name: macOS canvas scheme and window stack
summary: Filesystem-backed canvas shell with a custom URL scheme, safe file-serving handler, native panel lifecycle, and A2UI-aware auto-navigation hooks.

name: macOS companion CLI commands
summary: Thin macOS companion CLI that exposes discovery, direct gateway connect, and interactive wizard progression on top of the shared protocol stack.

name: Test sharding and release guardrails
summary: Project-scale test orchestration and release verification stack spanning vitest config fan-out, helper scripts and publish checks.

name: Vitest shard router and process-group runner
summary: Changed-file and explicit-target test router that picks the smallest viable Vitest lane, writes include files, and runs shards with cross-platform process cleanup.

name: Scoped Vitest config builder
summary: Reusable builder for repo-scoped Vitest configs with env-driven include narrowing, lane ordering, optional non-isolated runners, and extension-lane exclusions.

name: Isolated test-home and runtime reset harness
summary: Worker-safe test harness that stages a disposable home directory, sanitizes or copies live auth state when needed, and resets OpenClaw runtime caches between tests.

name: Gateway E2E spawn and probe harness
summary: Small integration-test harness that launches a minimal local gateway, connects node clients, posts hook payloads, and waits for node or chat state transitions.

name: Calver publish plan and dist-tag helper
summary: Bounded helper that parses OpenClaw-style calendar versions, compares release candidates, and computes npm publish tags plus mirror-tag auth policy.

name: Packed release and main-package guardrails
summary: Release validator that checks tarball contents, bundled extension metadata, pack budgets, appcast Sparkle floors, and npm package metadata before shipping.

name: Plugin release selection and ClawHub planning engine
summary: Git-range-aware selector for publishable plugin packages that validates package metadata and plans npm or ClawHub release candidates.

name: TypeScript public-surface topology analyzer
summary: CLI and analysis library that load a TypeScript program, enumerate public entrypoints, classify consumers, and rank exports by sharing, ownership, or dead surface.

name: Extension package-boundary scaffold
summary: Tsconfig helper and alias table that define the sanctioned import boundary for bundled extensions and generate opt-in package-boundary configs.

name: Gateway schema and Swift DTO codegen
summary: Codegen pair that emits a JSON schema for gateway frames and generated Swift protocol models from the shared protocol schema source.

name: A2UI versioned schema pack
summary: Vendored 0.8 and 0.9 JSON contracts for A2UI messages and standard component catalogs, suitable for validation or compatibility checks outside OpenClaw.

name: A2UI Lit message processor and surface-state builder
summary: Core 0.8 A2UI runtime that resolves server-to-client messages into surface state, component trees, path-aware data-model reads, and incremental updates.

name: A2UI Lit web-component renderer and custom registry
summary: Lit-based custom-element renderer that maps resolved A2UI component trees into standard web components and supports host-defined custom element overrides.

name: A2UI Angular host bridge
summary: Angular-specific host adapter that injects catalog and theme providers, dynamically instantiates components from a catalog, and reuses the Lit-side A2UI data processor.

name: A2UI 0.9 evaluation harness
summary: Genkit-backed evaluation pipeline that generates candidate A2UI payloads, validates them against the 0.9 schema, scores failures, and summarizes results per model.

name: OpenClaw A2UI bundle and canvas-host bridge
summary: OpenClaw-specific bundle and host bridge that compiles the vendored Lit renderer into one browser asset, injects host theming and action bridges, and serves the result from the canvas host.

name: Gateway tool-dispatch HTTP bridge
summary: Small bridge that authenticates POST /tools/invoke, resolves session-scoped tool availability, normalizes tool input failures, and executes one allowed tool without entering the full embedded-run loop.

name: Plugin manifest and setup registry bridge
summary: Discovery-time registry that loads plugin manifests, resolves contract ownership, and optionally imports setup-api sidecars to collect providers, CLI backends, config migrations, and auto-enable probes without booting the full runtime.

name: Managed task-flow state shell
summary: Persistent flow-state layer that mirrors or manages detached task progress with revisions, waiting state, blocked summaries, and owner-scoped lookup independent of any particular workflow DSL.

name: Lobster embedded workflow adapter
summary: OpenClaw-side adapter that runs or resumes Lobster workflows, maps approval pauses into managed task-flow state, and loads the actual workflow engine from the installed @clawdbot/lobster package at runtime.

name: QA scenario YAML pack and bootstrap loader
summary: Markdown-backed YAML scenario catalog with fenced qa-pack and qa-scenario blocks, Zod validation, and repo-relative loading for reusable test or workflow seed packs.

name: QA Lab mini workflow console
summary: Thin web console that polls repo-backed QA state, runs named scenarios, and renders chat, results, report, and event views without the full control UI shell.
